channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: What to know if you want to follow the Buckeyes to the Rose Bowl
item.description: It will be a warm welcome for Ohio State fans traveling to Pasadena after a frigid December home game at The Shoe.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Beachy, NewsNation

item.title: Andrew McCutchen signs 1-year, $5-million deal to stay with the Pittsburgh Pirates
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Andrew McCutchen is sticking around to see if the Pittsburgh Pirates can finish the climb back to contention. The five-time All-Star outfielder-turned-designated hitter has agreed to a one-year, $5 million deal, the club announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sabrina Ionescu is joining Unrivaled as the new 3-on-3 league's final player
item.description: Sabrina Ionescu is joining Unrivaled as the new 3-on-3 league's final player for this season. The Liberty star guard who helped New York win the first championship in franchise history earlier this year signed a historic agreement on Monday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Heisman winner Travis Hunter, fiancée delete social media amid rumors, criticism
item.description: Colorado Buffaloes star and Heisman Trophy winner Travis Hunter has deleted his social media, as did his fiancée Leanna Lenee, amid online criticism
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah blows 3-goal lead in shootout loss to Anaheim
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Robby Fabbri scored twice, and the Anaheim Ducks rallied from down three goals to defeat the Utah Hockey Club 5-4 in a shootout at Delta Center on Sunday. “I think we just didn't stray away from our game,” said Fabbri.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Eagles’ Jalen Hurts ruled out with a concussion in first quarter vs Commanders
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts was ruled out of Sunday's game against the Washington Commanders after sustaining a concussion in the first quarter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College Football Playoffs looks for good matchups after snoozy first round of blowouts
item.description: For those who dozed through it, or flipped to more compelling fare in the NFL, here's a brief recap of the rollout of the much-anticipated College Football Playoff. Average final score: Winners 36, Losers 17.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texans WR Tank Dell hospitalized after severe knee injury on TD catch vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans wide receiver Tank Dell was taken to the University of Kansas Medical Center and the team said he would remain overnight after sustaining a severe left knee injury while making a 30-yard touchdown catch in a 27-19 loss to the Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Guenther scores twice as Utah HC beats Minnesota, 2-1
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is in seventh heaven right about now. Dylan Guenther scored twice, including the game-winner at 10:01 of the third period, as Utah ran its road winning streak to seven in a row Friday night with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over the Minnesota Wild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trevor Baskin: From Division II star to Division I grinder
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — From Division II standout to Division I contributor, Trevor Baskin’s basketball journey has been anything but ordinary. After excelling at Colorado Mesa University, Baskin now competes at the highest level of college basketball with the Colorado Buffaloes.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Guentzel scores in 7 consecutive games, 1 shy of matching the franchise record in Tuesday's win against Blue Jackets
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Nick Paul had a goal and two assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to a 5-3 victory over the Columbus Blue Jackets. Jake Guentzel, Luke Glendening, Brayden Point and Mitchell Chaffee also scored for Tampa Bay, which has won five of six. Andrei Vasilevskiy finished with 28 saves.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cubs trade catcher Matt Thaiss to White Sox for cash considerations
item.description: The White Sox have acquired catcher Matt Thaiss from the Cubs in exchange for cash considerations, adding to their 40-man roster.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Florida Gators look to tie a bow on season in Gasparilla Bowl against Tulane
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Union Home Mortgage Gasparilla Bowl hosts the Tulane Green Wave (9-4) representing the American Athletic Conference and the University of Florida Gators (7-5) from the Southeastern Conference.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 84-year-old continues lawn bowling despite vision loss
item.description: On most days, you can find Ralph Trinity rolling with the Oaks North Lawn Bowling Club in Rancho Bernardo.
item.dc:creator: Troy Hirsch, Fox 5

item.title: Rays call for more money after Pinellas commissioners vote in favor of bonds for new stadium
item.description: Pinellas County commissioners voted in favor of approving bonds for the new Tampa Bay Rays stadium Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC
